title: buxus sempervirens f. rosmarinifolium
intro: Buxus sempervirens f. rosmarinifolium, also known as the boxwood with rosemary leaves, is a variety of the common boxwood plant (Buxus sempervirens) characterized by its needle-like, rosemary-scented foliage. Traditionally, Buxus sempervirens f. rosmarinifolium has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing stress and anxiety due to its adaptogenic properties, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antiviral activity. Buxus sempervirens f. rosmarinifolium can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used to treat various health issues.
